skill.name: react-native-mobile-development
skill.description: Build and troubleshoot React Native and Expo mobile apps with project setup, development commands, component patterns, platform handling, and common fixes.
skill.summary: Guide React Native and Expo app setup, workflows, components, and platform-specific troubleshooting.
skill.author: BabakBar

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command examples, documentation URLs, and several blocker patterns. Review found no prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, or data exfiltration; the weak cryptography and system reconnaissance matches are false positives. The remaining risk is that the skill permits Bash and file edits while recommending npm, npx, Expo, iOS, and Android commands that can execute project scripts or install third-party packages.

medium_findings.title: External Development Commands Require Review
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends npm, npx, Expo, iOS, and Android commands. This is appropriate for React Native work, but these commands can run package scripts, install dependencies, or modify native project files when executed by an agent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and relevant to the skill purpose. Risk depends on the target project and package sources, so this is a legitimate operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Broad Agent Tool Permissions
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Grep, and Glob permissions. These tools are useful for mobile development, but they allow command execution and filesystem changes if the host agent grants them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permissions are directly declared in the skill header. They are common for coding workflows, but users should understand the impact before using the skill on sensitive repositories.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Benign Network References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to React Native and Expo documentation. They do not include tracking parameters, credential collection, or instructions to send project data to an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links to expected official resources for this topic. No evidence found of network exfiltration or hidden download behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography match on the description line and system reconnaissance matches are not supported by the file content. The referenced lines describe mobile app development, running Android, and React Native styling guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the exact lines found no cryptographic algorithm use and no host reconnaissance command. The static signals appear to be keyword or markdown-context mistakes.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Triggered Command Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Several Ruby or shell backtick detections correspond to fenced TypeScript examples and inline Markdown formatting. These examples are instructional React Native snippets, not executable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched sections are Markdown fenced code blocks containing React Native component examples. They do not invoke a shell or process untrusted input.

dangerous_patterns.title: npm and npx Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to use npm and npx commands for Metro, Expo, iOS, Android, prebuild, and cache clearing workflows. These commands can execute local package scripts and dependency install hooks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present and executable in normal project contexts. They are not malicious by themselves, but they carry known supply-chain and local-script execution risk.

content.user_title: Build React Native Apps with Expo
content.value_statement: React Native projects often need precise setup, platform commands, and mobile-specific patterns. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Expo workflows, component structure, configuration, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Provides React Native and Expo setup commands for local development.
Shows a reusable mobile component pattern with TypeScript props and StyleSheet styles.
Explains platform-specific rendering and value selection for iOS and Android.
Lists common mobile best practices for performance, accessibility, responsiveness, navigation, and state.
Gives troubleshooting commands for Metro cache and native module issues.
Links users to official React Native and Expo documentation.
limitations: Does not include a complete app architecture or state management framework.
Does not validate package safety before npm or Expo commands are run.
Does not cover publishing to App Store Connect or Google Play Console.
Does not provide deep native Swift, Kotlin, or Gradle implementation guidance.

use_cases.title: Start an Expo Project
use_cases.description: Use the skill to choose commands, structure first components, and start the Metro development server.

use_cases.title: Fix Local Mobile Builds
use_cases.description: Use the troubleshooting guidance to clear Expo cache, prebuild native code, and review app configuration.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend engineer maintaining a React Native app

use_cases.title: Add Platform-Specific UI
use_cases.description: Use the platform patterns to adapt components, dimensions, and styles for iOS and Android behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up a Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a React Native app with Expo. Explain the commands I should run and the first files I should check.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Mobile Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable React Native component for my screen. Use TypeScript props, StyleSheet styles, and accessibility labels.

prompt_templates.title: Handle iOS and Android Differences
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React Native screen and recommend platform-specific adjustments for iOS and Android layout, spacing, and interaction behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Build and Native Module Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me diagnose a React Native or Expo build failure. Check Metro, native module setup, app configuration, and safe cleanup commands before changing files.

output_examples.input: I need to start an Expo app locally.
output_examples.output: A short setup plan with the Metro start command, simulator options, and files to inspect before changing configuration.

output_examples.input: My Android build fails after adding a package.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering dependency compatibility, Expo install guidance, native prebuild cleanup, and app configuration review.

output_examples.input: Make this screen behave well on iOS and Android.
output_examples.output: Platform-specific recommendations for layout height, shadows, elevation, touch targets, and conditional rendering.

best_practices: Review npm and npx commands before allowing an agent to execute them.
Use Expo-compatible package installation when working inside managed Expo projects.
Test important screens on both iOS and Android before accepting platform-specific changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run package installation commands in sensitive repositories without reviewing dependency sources.
Do not treat iOS simulator success as proof that Android behavior is correct.
Do not add platform checks when a shared React Native API can solve the problem cleanly.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does this skill create a full React Native application?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and patterns, but it is not a complete generated application template.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the suggested commands?
faq.answer: The commands are standard for React Native and Expo, but review them because npm and npx can execute project scripts.

faq.question: Does it support both iOS and Android?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes iOS and Android command examples and platform-specific React Native patterns.

faq.question: Does it cover Expo workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Expo start, package installation, prebuild, and cache-clearing guidance.

faq.question: Does it include official references?
faq.answer: Yes. It links to the React Native and Expo documentation sites.
